claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION documents four valid scopes with project evidence path requirements
evidence.why: Schema contract establishes the evidence scope field as project | external | user | unknown and states project facts require repo-relative paths.

evidence.summary: Four contract tests verify scope validation and project evidence path requirements
evidence.why: Tests confirm external-only evidence fails for existing project facts, URLs cannot be project evidence, and scope declaration is required.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules and result schema explicitly enumerate the four scope values
evidence.why: Prompt-level enforcement tells agents that evidence records must include scope and project facts require repo-relative paths.

evidence.summary: Readiness gates validate scope compliance and project evidence path requirements
evidence.why: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues prevent invalid or missing scope evidence from satisfying PRD requirements.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker both provided comprehensive project-scoped evidence with exact paths and line numbers. All four components—schema (router.ts:37-50), prompt (evidence-dispatch.ts:343-388), readiness contract (router.ts:438-530, 709-733), and tests (demand-router.test.ts:407-591)—explicitly distinguish and enforce the four evidence scopes. No conflicts between agents, no assumptions presented as facts, all evidence paths are usable, and no missing data remains.
